title: A Study to Investigate the Safety and Efficacy of SAR446523 Injected Subcutaneously in Adult Participants With Relapsed/Refractory Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Part A allows prior GPRC5D and BCMA exposure; Part B excludes prior GPRC5D. Part B requires refractory to IMiD, PI, anti-CD38, and BCMA. Inadequate organ/marrow function is an exclusion criterion but specific lab values are not detailed.

title: Integrative Therapies in Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Platelet threshold of 20x10^9/L applies specifically to patients receiving acupuncture. Hemodynamic instability and psychiatric disorder impairing competence are exclusion criteria.

title: Isatuximab in Combination With Novel Agents in RRMM - Master Protocol

eligibility.notes: Anti-CD38 exposure requirements vary by substudy (01/06: naive or prior with 12-month washout; 02/03: naive or non-refractory prior with 6-month washout; 04/05: mandatory prior exposure). Substudy 03 excludes prior belantamab mafodotin. Substudy 04 excludes active autoimmune disease, prior allo-HSCT, and CNS involvement. Substudy 06 excludes autoimmune hemolytic anemia, active GVHD, prior allo-HSCT, chronic EBV, HLH, and prior anti-CD47 therapy.

title: Study of ABBV-383 Assessing Adverse Events and Clinical Activity With Subcutaneous (SC) Injection in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Prior exposure to proteasome inhibitor, anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody, and immunomodulatory drug is required. Prior treatment with BCMA-targeting bispecific antibodies is excluded.

title: A Study to Assess Adverse Events of Intravenously (IV) Infused ABBV-383 in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Multi-arm trial with varying lines of therapy requirements: Arm A requires ≥3 LOT with PI/IMiD/CD38 exposure and excludes prior BCMA therapy; Arm B requires ≥2 LOT with prior BCMA-targeted therapy (ADC or CAR-T); Arm C requires ≥2 LOT with PI/IMiD/CD38 exposure and excludes rapidly progressing disease; Arm D requires 1-3 LOT with exposure to at least one of PI/IMiD/CD38 and excludes rapidly progressing disease.

title: A Study of JNJ-79635322 in Combination With Daratumumab or Pomalidomide for Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Multiple treatment regimens with varying prior therapy requirements: Regimen A1 requires 1-3 prior lines with PI and IMiD exposure; Regimen B requires ≥1 line with PI/lenalidomide and lenalidomide refractoriness OR ≥2 lines with PI/lenalidomide; Regimens A2, C, D, E require newly diagnosed treatment-naive status. Prior anti-CD38 therapy excluded within 90 days for Regimen A, within 21 days for Regimen B. Prior CAR-T and CD3-redirecting therapy excluded. Autologous SCT must be >12 weeks prior; allogeneic SCT must be >6 months prior.

title: Study Assessing Activity of Intravenous (IV) Etentamig Monotherapy Versus Standard Available Therapies in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients must be eligible to receive investigator's choice standard available therapy. Prior BCMA-targeted therapy is explicitly exclusionary.

title: Study of CAR-BCMA, a Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cell (CAR-T) Therapy Directed Against BCMA in Subjects With Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: CAR-T cell therapy trial requiring at least 3 prior treatment regimens. Peripheral blood plasma cells must be <5%. Cardiac ejection fraction ≥45% required. Belantamab mafotodin is allowed prior therapy despite investigational agent restriction.

title: MagnetisMM-32: A Study to Learn About the Study Medicine Called Elranatamab in People With Multiple Myeloma (MM) That Has Come Back After Taking Other Treatments (Including Prior Treatment With an Anti-CD38 Antibody and Lenalidomide)

eligibility.notes: Patients must have received prior CD38 antibody and lenalidomide. Prior BCMA-directed therapy or CD3-redirecting therapy excluded. Progressive disease or failure to respond to last line required.

title: Study With Elranatamab Versus Lenalidomide in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma After Transplant

eligibility.notes: Post-ASCT maintenance trial in newly diagnosed MM with MRD monitoring. Randomization timing: within 120 days of ASCT, or within 60 days of consolidation and 7 months from ASCT. Patients must achieve PR or better at randomization.

title: A Study to Compare Iberdomide Maintenance Versus Lenalidomide Maintenance Therapy Following Autologous Stem Cell Transplant in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients must have achieved at least partial response after ASCT and be within 12 months (single transplant) or 15 months (tandem transplant) from induction initiation. Smoldering myeloma, solitary plasmacytoma, and nonsecretory myeloma are excluded.

title: Open-label Study Comparing Iberdomide, Daratumumab and Dexamethasone (IberDd) Versus Daratumumab, Bortezomib, and Dexamethasone (DVd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM)

eligibility.notes: Patient must have documented disease progression during or after their last anti-myeloma regimen. Prior iberdomide therapy is an exclusion criterion.

title: A Study to Examine the Effects of Novel Therapy Linvoseltamab in Combination With Other Cancer Treatments for Adult Patients With Multiple Myeloma That is Resistant to Current Standard of Care Treatments

eligibility.notes: Multi-cohort trial with cohort-specific requirements. Cohort 2 expansion allows BCMA CAR-T if ≥12 weeks prior. Cohorts 7-8 have active autoimmune exclusion (within 2 years). Cohort 4 excludes grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy. Cardiac ejection fraction must be ≥40%. Patients must be off prior anti-myeloma therapy ≥21 days or 5 half-lives.

title: A Study to Learn About the Effects of the Combination of Elranatamab (PF-06863135), Daratumumab, Lenalidomide or Elranatamab and Lenalidomide Compared With Daratumumab, Lenalidomide, and Dexamethasone in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma Who Are Not Candidates for Transplant

eligibility.notes: RRMM participants must have received 1-2 prior lines including at least one IMiD and one PI. BCMA and anti-CD38 therapy excluded within 6 months for RRMM. NDMM must be transplant-ineligible or transplant-deferred. NDMM participants may receive minimal corticosteroids only. SMM explicitly excluded.

title: A Study of Daratumumab, Bortezomib, Lenalidomide and Dexamethasone (DVRd) Followed by Ciltacabtagene Autoleucel Versus Daratumumab, Bortezomib, Lenalidomide and Dexamethasone (DVRd) Followed by Autologous Stem Cell Transplant (ASCT) in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Treatment-naive patients only (prior therapy limited to short course of corticosteroids). High-dose therapy and ASCT are part of intended initial treatment plan. Exclusion for stroke or seizure within 6 months.

title: A Clinical Trial of Four Medicines (Elranatamab Plus Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone or Maplirpacept) in People With Relapsed Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Part 1 (1-3 prior LOT) excludes prior BCMA therapy; Part 2 (≥3 prior LOT) requires refractoriness to IMiD, PI, and anti-CD38 but excludes prior anti-BCMA (except CAR-T) and CD47-SIRP alpha therapy. Induction + ASCT + consolidation/maintenance counts as 1 line of therapy.

title: Phase 3 Study of Teclistamab in Combination With Lenalidomide and Teclistamab Alone Versus Lenalidomide Alone in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma as Maintenance Therapy Following Autologous Stem Cell Transplantation

eligibility.notes: Patient must have achieved at least partial response to induction therapy without evidence of progression. Prior BCMA-directed therapy and CAR-T therapy excluded. Patients intolerant to lenalidomide or discontinued due to lenalidomide-related AE are excluded.

title: A Study of JNJ-68284528, a Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cell (CAR-T) Therapy Directed Against B-cell Maturation Antigen (BCMA) in Participants With Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Multiple cohorts with varying eligibility: Cohort A (1-3 prior LOT, lenalidomide refractory); Cohort B (1 prior LOT, progression ≤12mo post-ASCT); Cohort C (prior PI, IMiD, anti-CD38, BCMA); Cohort D (NDMM, 4-8 cycles); Cohort E (NDMM, no prior therapy, high-risk); Cohort F (NDMM, VGPR+ response); Cohorts G-H (NDMM, ASCT considerations). BCMA and CAR-T prohibited for Cohorts A,B,C,D,F.

title: A Study of Teclistamab in Combination With Daratumumab and Lenalidomide (Tec-DR) and Talquetamab in Combination With Daratumumab and Lenalidomide (Tal-DR) in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients must be newly diagnosed and not ASCT-eligible due to advanced age, comorbidities, or deferral of high-dose chemotherapy. Prior therapy limited to short course corticosteroids (≤160 mg dexamethasone equivalent) and cumulative systemic corticosteroids <20 mg dexamethasone within 14 days before randomization.

title: A Long-term Study for Participants Previously Treated With Ciltacabtagene Autoleucel

eligibility.notes: This appears to be a follow-up study for participants who have already received cilta-cel (BCMA CAR-T) in a prior company-sponsored trial. No additional eligibility criteria specified beyond prior cilta-cel exposure and informed consent.

title: A Study of Selinexor (Seli) + Low-dose Dexamethasone (LDD) in Penta-refractory Multiple Myeloma (MM), Seli and Bortezomib + LDD in Triple-class Refractory MM.

eligibility.notes: Prior exposure to SINE compounds (including selinexor) is exclusionary. SVd arm has additional exclusion for Grade >2 peripheral neuropathy or Grade ≥2 with pain. Multiple arms with varying minimum prior therapy requirements (3-4 lines depending on protocol version and arm).

title: Cyclosporine in Combination With Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone in Relapsed Multiple Myeloma Refractory to Carfilzomib and High Expression of PPIA Gene in Myeloma Cells

eligibility.notes: Patient must be carfilzomib-refractory (failed to achieve minor response after 2 cycles or refractory to treatment) and must have high PPIA expression (>1.2 UMI per cell) by scRNA sequencing. Prior allogeneic transplantation excluded.

title: A Phase 1b/2 Study of Sonrotoclax (BGB-11417) as Monotherapy and in Various Combinations With Dexamethasone Plus Carfilzomib, Dexamethasone Plus Daratumumab, and Dexamethasone Plus Pomalidomide in Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: t(11;14) translocation must be confirmed by FISH testing. Trial has multiple cohorts with varying LOT requirements (Part 1/2 Cohorts 1-2: ≥3 LOT; Cohorts 3-5: ≥1 LOT; Cohorts 6-7: 1-3 LOT). Carfilzomib prior exposure allowed if not refractory.

title: Study of HBI0101 (NXC-201) CAR-T Therapy in Multiple Myeloma and Light-Chain Amyloidosis

eligibility.notes: Trial includes both multiple myeloma (RRMM) and light-chain amyloidosis patients with different prior therapy requirements. MM patients require exposure to ≥2 prior lines (PI, IMiD, or anti-CD38), or first relapse within 18 months. Amyloidosis patients require ≥1 prior line with PI or anti-CD38, or insufficient response criteria. LVEF <40% is exclusionary. Active infections (HIV, Hepatitis B/C, CMV) excluded.

title: A Study to Evaluate Mezigdomide in Combination With Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone (MeziKD) Versus Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone (Kd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (SUCCESSOR-2)

eligibility.notes: Patient must have achieved minimal response or better to at least 1 prior therapy. Allogeneic stem cell transplant at any time or autologous stem cell transplant within 12 weeks of study initiation is excluded.

title: A Study to Evaluate Mezigdomide, Bortezomib and Dexamethasone (MEZIVd) Versus Pomalidomide, Bortezomib and Dexamethasone (PVd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM)

eligibility.notes: Participants must have achieved minimal response or better to at least one prior therapy. PI-refractory defined as progression during or within 60 days of last PI dose, with exceptions for maintenance therapy regimens.

title: A Study to Compare the Efficacy and Safety of BMS-986393 Versus Standard Regimens in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory and Lenalidomide-refractory Multiple Myeloma (QUINTESSENTIAL-2)

eligibility.notes: Prior exposure to lenalidomide required. Patients with solitary plasmacytomas or non-secretory myeloma without measurable disease are excluded.

title: A Study Evaluating the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Activity of the Combination of Cevostamab and Elranatamab in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (R/R MM)

eligibility.notes: Prior allogeneic SCT is exclusionary. Known/suspected chronic CMV and/or EBV infection, acute or chronic HBV/HCV, and HIV seropositivity are exclusionary. History of PML, HLH, or MAS also excluded.

title: A Trial to Learn How Well Linvoseltamab Works Compared to the Combination of Elotuzumab, Pomalidomide and Dexamethasone for Adult Participants With Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients with only 1 prior line must be lenalidomide refractory. Specific regional requirements: Israel requires prior CD38 antibody; EU/UK require 2-4 prior lines including CD38 antibody. Cardiac ejection fraction must be ≥40%. Prior allogeneic transplant requires 6-month washout and off immunosuppressants for 6 weeks; prior autologous transplant requires 12-week washout.

title: A Study Comparing Teclistamab Monotherapy Versus Pomalidomide, Bortezomib, Dexamethasone (PVd) or Carfilzomib, Dexamethasone (Kd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patient must have received minimum 2 consecutive cycles of anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody and 2 consecutive cycles of lenalidomide in prior therapy. Progressive disease or failure to achieve response to last line required. High cumulative corticosteroid exposure (≥140 mg prednisone equivalent within 14 days) is exclusionary.

title: A Study Comparing Talquetamab in Combination With Daratumumab or in Combination With Daratumumab and Pomalidomide Versus Daratumumab in Combination With Pomalidomide and Dexamethasone in Participants With Multiple Myeloma That Returns After Treatment or is Resistant to Treatment

eligibility.notes: Patients with 1 prior line must be lenalidomide-refractory; those with ≥2 prior lines must be lenalidomide-exposed. Patients with CD38-refractory disease are excluded. Maximum corticosteroid dose of ≥140 mg prednisone equivalent within 14 days before first dose is excluded.

title: A Study Comparing Talquetamab Plus Pomalidomide, Talquetamab Plus Teclistamab, and Elotuzumab, Pomalidomide, and Dexamethasone or Pomalidomide, Bortezomib, and Dexamethasone in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Myeloma Who Have Received an Anti-CD38 Antibody and Lenalidomide

eligibility.notes: Patients must have documented progressive disease or failure to achieve minimal response to last line of therapy. High-dose corticosteroids (≥140 mg prednisone equivalent in 14 days) are excluded. Stroke, TIA, or seizure within 6 months, and major surgery within 2 weeks are exclusionary.

title: A Study of Talquetamab in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Cohort A and C: CAR-T or T cell redirection therapy at any time excluded. Cohort B, D, E: T cell redirection therapy within 3 months excluded. High-dose corticosteroids (≥140 mg prednisone equivalent) within 14 days before first dose excluded. Stroke or seizure within 6 months excluded.

title: Predicting Progression of Developing Myeloma in a High-Risk Screened Population

eligibility.notes: This is a screening/prevention trial for high-risk individuals. Patients with diagnosed plasma cell dyscrasias (MGUS, SMM, MM, Waldenström) are excluded. Enrollment requires either age ≥30 with first-degree relative with plasma cell dyscrasia, or age ≥18 with 2+ first/second-degree relatives with plasma cell dyscrasia.
